The 98th Academy Awards red carpet kicked off in style on Sunday, with Hollywood's brightest stars descending upon the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles wearing their most glamorous looks. Kylie Jenner was every inch the bombshell as she channeled Jessica Rabbit in a sultry, metallic red gown while showing up to support boyfriend Timothee Chalamet, who is nominated for Best Actor.  The reality TV star commanded attention in the ultra-revealing gown, which featured a key-shaped cutout just at the base of the plunging halter neckline.

Grammar school girl and university student dead in 'nightclub-linked' meningitis outbreak in Kent: Eleven more youngsters 'seriously ill' in hospital

A meningitis outbreak said to be linked to a nightclub has killed two young people and left 11 others seriously ill. The victims, who have not been named, are thought to be aged between 17 and 21. One was confirmed as a student at the University of Kent while the other was a sixth-form pupil at Queen Elizabeth's Grammar School in nearby Faversham.

A Guardian opinion piece has prompted outrage after it described the opening of a Gail's as an 'act of heavy-handed high-street aggression' against Palestinians. The article, published on Saturday, features an interview with a Palestinian cafe owner in Archway, north London where a new branch of Gail's was recently established. Gail's was founded by Israeli baker Gail Mejia in the 1990s before being rapidly expanded by Israeli entrepreneur Ran Avidan from 2005 - but both are no longer linked to the company.

ROBERT HARDMAN: As the Ayatollah's apologists chanted 'From the River to the sea', I heard strains of 'God Save the King' floating across the Thames

We operate from a place of love!' yelled rapper Bobby Vylan to appreciative nods from the crowd below him. Moments later, he emphasised the point: 'Everything we do stems from love!' At which point, he rounded off his sermon on a suitably tender note. 'I'd just like to conclude with this: Death! Death! To the IDF!' The crowd around me gleefully joined in with Vylan's loving call for the slaughter of the Israeli Defence Forces over and over, waving, in equal measure, Palestinian flags, Iranian flags and posters of recently-terminated Iranian leader Ali Khamenei, billed as 'The Great Martyr. Our Pride. Our Leader'. A group of teenage girls in front of me were literally jumping up and down for joy.

A flamboyant entrepreneur who made toys used by millions of children around the world for 25 years took his own life when the business began to go under. David Strang, 52, made toys including supersized bouncy balls and indoor boomerangs that were sold at outlets including Hamleys, Walmart, Sports Direct and Amazon. Strang also excelled as a gatecrasher, sneaking his way into events like the Oscars, the Baftas and the Brits - and over the years took selfies with the likes of Halle Berry, Kate Hudson, Tom Jones, Dizzee Rascal, Anne-Marie and Steven Tyler.

If you can’t read it the DR says anyone suffering hot flushes should see their DR as soon as possible. While a common menopausal symptom in women in men it could be a sign of blood cancer. Often it is accompanied with fatigue or lack of appetite. Hot flushes could also be a sign of TB. Other symptoms include a cough that lasts more than three weeks and a high temperature once diagnosed can be treated with antibiotics. Sometimes hot flushes in men can be a sign of low testosterone also known as male hypogonadism

The Prince of Wales has shared a never-before-seen photo of the late Princess Diana in a poignant Mother's Day message. On Sunday night the women and their colleagues were hailed 'American heroes' by US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth.   • share The beds that make your sleeping arrangements look 'tacky' revealed

I am retired so much more time. There is quite a lot of furniture I cannot move unaided. All of these areas really do collect a lot of dust and hair especially if you have cats like I do. I do what I can, and know I cannot keep everything clean. I have things that help keep it down. Under beds and the sofas, one of those flat swifter style mops, 5 minutes every week, and wet once a month. On top of wardrobes and kitchen appliances and for gaps everywhere, a long thin paddle covered with a damp microfibre sleeve, every couple of months. Most of the lights have glass snap in shades, they get cleaned when I see dead insects. This is getting harder, my balance is not so good on steps. Every 2 or 3 years my home is painted, all white, and it is always the same people, so I pay a little extra for their time to allow me I clean as they move stuff. It is easier to clean a little everyday, especially bathrooms. But when I was working these corners were barely tackled and the children thrived!

According to 'Queen of Clean' Lynsey Crombie, there are several blind spots that people frequently forget about, despite them being vital in giving a home that extra sparkle.

Ant McPartlin shares a passionate kiss with wife Anne-Marie Corbett as he celebrates his beloved Newcastle's win at Stamford Bridge alongside Declan Donnelly and his wife Ali Astall

Ant McPartlin shared a passionate kiss with his wife Anne-Marie Corbett as he celebrated his beloved Newcastle's win at Stamford Bridge on Saturday alongside Declan Donnelly and his wife Ali Astall. The Geordie duo enjoyed a double date with their partners as Newcastle took on Chelsea and won 1-0. Football fan Ant, 50, couldn't resist a cheeky kiss as his team scored and he celebrated in the crowds with Anne-Marie, who wrapped up warm in a brown padded coat.

The other Strictly curse: Pro dancers have been blighted by debt, mental health battles and legal wrangles after leaving the show - as the five stars axed in new bloodbath face an uncertain future amid money woes

The 'Strictly curse' is a famous pop culture phenomenon, with most able to pinpoint a celeb who caused a stir by waltzing off with their dance partner.  Yet many are unfamiliar with the other Strictly curse - that has seen a series of professional dancers fall on hard times after leaving the show.  The Strictly pros dedicate half their year to living and breathing Strictly mania, yet their short-term contracts mean they are always at risk of being unceremoniously dropped.

A line of tourists touch up their makeup before strutting across a rooftop in Rio de Janeiro's largest favela, posing for a drone as it zooms out to show dramatic aerial views of the hillside community. Set to an infectious beat, the video of the Rocinha favela has exploded on social media at a time when the city is drawing record tourist numbers. It has also created a sense of unease, with dozens of comments accusing visitors of glamourising poverty and crime in a low-income community dominated by drug traffickers.

Harry Styles took a scathing swipe at Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor during his Saturday Night Live monologue, after disgraced ex-Prince was arrested last month. The singer, 32, returned for a second stint fronting the NBC show after his fourth album soared to the top of the charts. During his monologue, Harry referenced his lengthy absence from the spotlight, quipping he'd become 'tremendously boring', before quipping his quiet spell was welcomed when compared to Andrew public fall from grace.

  • John was very sick for a long time before he died. She would've known that the end was coming a long while ago; the ups and downs of a long-term illness are hard on a relationship. She stood by him through all of that. She would've mentally prepared herself for the inevitable. It's a very different loss from a sudden, unexpected death. Both awful but Lorna had time to adjust. She deserves to be out with friends. Anyone who has gone through the grieving process knows that one minute you are OK, and then the loss and sadness come over you like a wave. Distracting yourself from the pain of loss doesn't mean to say you aren't feeling it.

  • Greg James is a national treasurer, his show and the breakfast team saved me through Covid (a time in which he was really struggling too but you would never have known). He did a couple of really difficult shows, one after the Queen died and another after Liam Payne died. He managed to get the tone of both of those spot on whilst most importantly letting people know there is no right/wrong way to feel and however it is you are feeling you are not alone. His openness in talking about mental health on air, including his own, is refreshing and very very needed. If anyone can do this ridiculous challenge it's Greg, I have no doubt about that.

Japan resident reveals how to beat the cherry blossom crowds

To help Japan visitors get the most out of cherry blossom season this spring, Tokyo local Takao Nishina has shared ten tips and tricks, which include arriving early to key hotspots and heading out for nighttime strolls for an alternative view of the stunning soft pink trees. The natural phenomenon takes place over the course of two weeks, beginning in the southern tip in Okinawa, before sweeping north, finishing in Hokkaido.
  • comments
  • share Japan resident reveals how to beat the cherry blossom crowds

Kinda puts a whole new spin on "Take your daughter to work day", doesn't it?

Email

Sweet little mite.

A female sloth named Reina delivered a healthy cub while suspended 14 meters above the ground. In a display of instinct that mirrored life in the wild, Reina remained hanging from the branches of a towering tree in her enclosure throughout the labour. The newborn cub, measuring approximately 20 centimetres long, was seen clinging tightly to its mother's fur moments after entering the world. Baby sloths are born fully developed, with claws that allow them to cling to their mother from the very first second of life.
  • 3 comments
  • share Just hanging out! Reina the two-toed sloth holds her newborn baby
